jobs.description:
We are seeking an experienced, customer-focused Principal Technical Program Manager to lead the development of innovative solutions in brand measurement. This role will play a pivotal part in helping advertisers measure, analyze, and optimize their brand-building campaigns across Amazon Ads. By delivering performance reporting and actionable AI driven insights, our team empowers global advertisers to connect their media investments with real customer outcomes. If you’re passionate about data-driven decision-making and developing solutions that impact millions of customers, this is the role for you!

Key job responsibilities
As a Principal TPM, you will own the planning, execution, and delivery of large-scale technical programs that span multiple engineering teams, product organizations, and business stakeholders. You'll work at the intersection of advertising technology, machine learning, and distributed systems to solve ambiguous problems that shape the future of digital advertising.

- Own end-to-end program management for strategic advertising technology initiatives spanning multiple teams and organizations
- Drive technical strategy and roadmap alignment across engineering, product, science, and business teams
- Lead cross-functional teams through complex technical challenges in areas such as ad serving, campaign management, measurement, and optimization
- Establish program governance, metrics, and reporting mechanisms to ensure visibility and accountability at the VP level
- Identify and mitigate technical and operational risks across large-scale distributed systems serving billions of ad requests daily
- Partner with engineering leaders to influence architecture decisions and ensure scalability, reliability, and performance
- Build and maintain relationships with senior stakeholders across Amazon Ads, AWS, and Retail organizations
- Drive operational excellence through process improvements, automation, and best practices
- Mentor and develop other TPMs across the organization
- Represent Amazon Ads technical programs in executive-level reviews and planning sessions

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 5+ years of program or project management experience
- Experience using data and metrics to determine and drive improvements
- Experience owning program strategy, end to end delivery, and communicating results to senior leadership
- Experience in supply chain program, project, or automation related product management, or leading an implementation of a product
- Experience managing and negotiating contracts and vendor relationships
- Experience that includes strong analytical skills, attention to detail, and effective communication abilities
- Proficiency with MRP/ERP systems and BOM-driven planning processes
jobs.description:
As Program Manager, Supply Chain, you will own and drive the full supply chain program — from strategic sourcing and supplier development through demand planning, inventory management, and materials fulfillment for production. You'll serve as the strategic program leader connecting engineering, manufacturing, and our contract manufacturer, ensuring parts are available, suppliers are performing, and the business has the visibility it needs to make informed decisions. This is a high-impact program management role at a critical stage of the company's growth.

Key job responsibilities
Strategic Sourcing & Procurement
- Own vendor selection, qualification, and onboarding for new components and materials across mechanical, electrical, and electromechanical categories
- Negotiate pricing, lead times, MOQs, and contractual terms with suppliers
- Identify and qualify alternate/secondary sources for critical and sole-sourced components to reduce supply risk
- Partner with engineering during NPI to drive component selection toward supply-resilient, cost-effective, and readily available parts
- Manage supplier relationships, scorecards, and performance reviews

Demand Planning & Inventory Management
- Develop and maintain demand forecasts in collaboration with product, engineering, and leadership; translate forecasts into material requirements and procurement plans
- Manage inventory Levels to balance availability against carrying cost and obsolescence risk
- Track and communicate parts availability status, shortages, and risk to production schedules; drive resolution of critical shortages

Production Materials & CM Coordination
- Serve as the primary supply chain interface with the contract manufacturer — coordinating materials planning, consigned vs. turnkey decisions, and kitting
- Monitor supplier on-time delivery and component yield; drive corrective actions for chronic performance issues
- Support subassembly and system builds with real-time materials visibility and escalation paths
- Own the end-to-end PO lifecycle from issuance through receipt and reconciliation

Operations & Process Development
- Build and scale supply chain processes, tools, and systems appropriate from concept to production — including ERP/MRP setup or optimization
- Lead cost reduction initiatives through negotiation, re-sourcing, and design-to-cost partnerships with engineering
- Manage logistics and freight strategy for inbound materials and outbound finished goods

About the team
Fauna Robotics, an Amazon company, is building capable, safe, and genuinely delightful robots for everyday life. Our goal is simple: make robots people actually want to live and interact with in everyday human spaces.
We believe that future won’t arrive until building for robotics becomes far more accessible. Today, too much effort is spent reinventing the fundamentals. We’re changing that by developing tightly integrated hardware and software systems that make it faster, safer, and more intuitive to create real-world robotic products.
Our work spans the full stack: mechanical design, control systems, dynamic modeling, and intelligent software. The focus is not just functionality, but experience. We’re building robots that feel responsive, expressive, and genuinely useful.
At Fauna, you’ll work at the frontier of this space, helping define how robots move, manipulate, and interact with people in natural environments. It’s an opportunity to solve hard problems across hardware and software with a team focused on making robotics accessible and joyful to build.
If you care about making robotics real for everyone and building systems that are as delightful as they are capable, we’re interested in hearing from you.
jobs.description_short: As Program Manager, Supply Chain, you will own and drive the full supply chain program  — from strategic sourcing and supplier development through demand planning, inventory management, and materials fulfillment

jobs.description:
Join Amazon’s growing RME (Reliability, Maintenance & Engineering) team as a Maintenance Supervisor at our Craigieburn site, leading a team of 2 Technicians (fitters and electricians).

Our Reliability Maintenance Engineering (RME) team is central to Amazon's commitment to innovation. As Amazon evolves and adapts, this team makes sure that the tools and technologies we use do as well. As a Maintenance Supervisor you'll help us stay one step ahead, adopting the latest technologies and identifying new and efficient ways of working. In this role, you’ll pay close attention to our processes to help maintain our high standards, and you’ll put in place upgrades to take that standard even higher.

Learn more about Amazon RME in Australia

Key job responsibilities
- Lead and Motivate: Supervise a team of maintenance technicians, providing strategic guidance and professional development
- Develop and implement comprehensive preventative maintenance programs and facility compliance strategies
- Act as an ambassador for safety within the team and to promote safe working across the site
- Install, maintain, and troubleshoot complex facility systems and automated equipment
- Cross-Functional Collaboration: Build strong relationships across organizational levels and participate in strategic leadership meetings

A day in the life
Your day will be a dynamic blend of hands-on technical work and strategic leadership. You'll lead a team of technicians (fitters and electricians), troubleshoot complex equipment challenges, and develop proactive maintenance strategies that keep our cutting-edge systems performing at their peak.

jobs.description:
Application deadline: Apr 13, 2026

Passionate about Big Data and Distributed Systems? Interested in building new state-of-the-art products using AWS Services? Be part of a team of industry leading experts that operates one of the largest big data analytics systems at Amazon. We provide privacy safe measurement analytics to all of Amazon's advertising partners.

Candidates for this position should be creative, demonstrated leaders with superior analytical abilities and a strong drive to deliver results. This opportunity requires deep technical, problem-solving, and communication skills. We encourage applications from candidates with diverse backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives.




Key job responsibilities
- Define and drive the multi-year vision for a big data platform.
- Build, manage, and develop truly innovative engineers including making hiring recommendations and guiding and mentoring engineers and product and program managers within and outside of the team.
- Deliver robust and scalable solutions toward your vision, invent or incorporate in new technologies when needed.
- Ensure high quality design reviews that meet business and architectural goals and drive critical feedback on architecture and design issues.
- Oversee resource allocation to ensure appropriate business and staff development goals are met.
- Build relationships with software development managers, engineers, product managers and technical program managers across upstream and downstream teams to influence product development for advertising traffic data.
- Participate in strategic planning to achieve technical and business objectives and interact with customers, other leaders, and your team to construct your plan.

About the team
The Advertising Analytics Platform is hiring SDMs!
Are you passionate about using Big Data to grow new businesses? Global advertisers rely on our team's performance insights to drive future investment in Amazon's Advertising Platform. We’re looking for a strong Software Engineer Manager that can combine open source technologies such as Hadoop, Hive, Spark and Presto, as well as AWS services like Elastic Map Reduce (EMR), Kinesis and DynamoDB to build the next generation of our analytics and visualization platform. You’ll be the single-threaded owner of video advertising traffic metrics. If this sounds interesting, we’d love to hear from you!

jobs.description:
About Maintenance at Amazon

Operations is at the heart of Amazon’s business. We are known for our speed, accuracy, and exceptional service. Our buildings deliver tens of thousands of products to hundreds of countries worldwide, every day. Reliability Maintenance & Engineering (RME) are the business partners that work tirelessly behind the scenes to make it all happen. We drive continuous improvement and maintain all of the Robotics and Material Handling Equipment (MHE) to ensure our customers are met with the Amazon smile. Come join us on our journey!

About the Role

As a Mechatronics & Robotics Technician, you will use your skills to support the Operations Maintenance team in repairing and maintaining material handling equipment and pneumatic systems throughout the building. You will have the opportunity to install, maintain, and repair automated packaging and distribution equipment. In addition to developing your skills, you will mentor junior technicians to grow in their roles.

What Do We Offer?

Amazon offers competitive compensation packages including comprehensive healthcare benefits starting on Day 1, matching 401(k) program, and up to 20 weeks of paid parental leave. But wait, there is more: We provide lightweight fire-resistant daily wear and composite toe safety shoes for your safety!

Here is what you can expect with the Reliability & Maintenance Engineering (RME) selection process for Mechatronics & Robotics Technicians: (Timeframe: Approximately 30-days)

Step 1: Congratulations! You successfully submitted your application!

Step 2: Amazon Recruiting will review your application and invite the most qualified candidate to complete a pre-employment assessment.

Step 3: If you meet the role qualifications, you will receive an invitation from Amazon Recruiting (do-not-reply@criteriacorp.com) to begin your Criteria assessment. You have 5 days to complete.

Step 4: Once you successfully meet the pre-employment assessment requirements, you will be invited to complete a video interview. Your video interview invitation will be from HireVue (interviews@hirevue.com) and you will have 7 days to record your interview.

Step 5: Your video interview will be reviewed by the Amazon Hiring Team and you will be notified of next steps via email within 5 business days.

Key job responsibilities
Key job responsibilities include, but are not limited to:


- You will promote a safe working environment by following all safety procedures
- You will complete preventative maintenance routines with proper documentation
- You will troubleshoot electrical and mechanical problems related to all Material Handling Equipment (MHE) which includes belts, motors, photo-eyes, relays, and more
- You will maintain a positive working relationship across all of the Amazon Operations facility
- You must be able to move up to 49lbs, stand and walk during shifts lasting up to 12 hours, climb ladders and gangways safely, and without limitation, as well as perform regular bending, lifting, stretching, and reaching both below the waist and above the head

Please note that while we strive to accommodate employees' preferences for shift assignments, due to operational requirements and scheduling constraints, we cannot guarantee a specific shift assignment.

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Experience in written and verbal communication with the ability to present complex technical information in a clear and concise manner to executives and non-technical leaders
- 5+ years of quality engineering experience in a hardware product company (consumer electronics, robotics, medical devices, or similar)
- Hands-on experience with quality systems in a contract manufacturing environment
- Proficiency with inspection tools, metrology, and functional test methods for electromechanical assemblies
- Experience authoring work instructions, ATPs, and inspection criteria for production
jobs.description:
As a Senior Quality Engineer, you will own and drive quality systems across the full hardware development and production lifecycle — from early prototypes through subassembly builds and into volume production at our contract manufacturer. You'll work closely with our mechanical, electrical, embedded software, and manufacturing teams to build robust quality processes, define test coverage strategies, and ensure our products meet reliability, safety, and performance requirements.

Key job responsibilities
Quality Systems & Process
- Develop, implement, and maintain quality management systems (QMS) appropriate for a hardware startup in early production
- Establish and own incoming inspection, in-process inspection, and final inspection criteria and procedures
- Define and drive First Article Inspection (FAI) processes with our contract manufacturer
- Create and manage Non-Conformance Reports (NCRs), CAPAs, and deviation/waiver processes

Documentation & Work Instructions
- Author and maintain clear, accurate work instructions, assembly procedures, and inspection checklists for the production floor
- Ensure documentation is version-controlled and synchronized with engineering changes
- Partner with engineering to enforce Design for Manufacturability (DFM) and Design for Testability (DFT) principles

Contract Manufacturer Partnership
- Serve as a key quality interface with contractors — conducting audits, reviewing quality plans, and driving resolution of production issues
- Travel to CM facilities as needed to support builds and resolve quality escapes
- Establish quality metrics and reporting cadences with CM leadership

Reliability & Compliance
- Lead or support DFMEA/PFMEA activities to proactively identify and mitigate failure modes
- Support product safety and compliance activities (UL, CE, RoHS, UN38.3 for battery, etc.)
- Drive reliability testing planning and execution, including environmental stress screening (ESS) and accelerated life testing (ALT)

About the team
Fauna Robotics, an Amazon company, is building capable, safe, and genuinely delightful robots for everyday life. Our goal is simple: make robots people actually want to live and interact with in everyday human spaces.
We believe that future won’t arrive until building for robotics becomes far more accessible. Today, too much effort is spent reinventing the fundamentals. We’re changing that by developing tightly integrated hardware and software systems that make it faster, safer, and more intuitive to create real-world robotic products.
Our work spans the full stack: mechanical design, control systems, dynamic modeling, and intelligent software. The focus is not just functionality, but experience. We’re building robots that feel responsive, expressive, and genuinely useful.
At Fauna, you’ll work at the frontier of this space, helping define how robots move, manipulate, and interact with people in natural environments. It’s an opportunity to solve hard problems across hardware and software with a team focused on making robotics accessible and joyful to build.
If you care about making robotics real for everyone and building systems that are as delightful as they are capable, we’re interested in hearing from you.

jobs.company_name: Amazon Servicos de Varejo do Brasil Ltda.
jobs.description:
Do you want to solve real customer problems through innovative technology? Do you enjoy working on scalable services in a collaborative team environment? Do you want to see your code directly impact millions of customers worldwide?
At Amazon, we hire the best minds in technology to innovate and build on behalf of our customers. Customer obsession is part of our company DNA, which has made us one of the world's most beloved brands.
Our Software Development Engineers (SDEs) use modern technology to solve complex problems while seeing their work's impact first-hand. The challenges SDEs solve at Amazon are meaningful and influence millions of customers, sellers, and products globally. We seek individuals passionate about creating new products, features, and services while managing ambiguity in an environment where development cycles are measured in weeks, not years.
At Amazon, we believe in ownership at every level. As an SDE-I, you'll own the entire lifecycle of your code - from design through deployment and ongoing operations. This ownership mindset, combined with our commitment to operational excellence, ensures we deliver the highest quality solutions for our customers.
We're looking for curious minds who think big and want to define tomorrow's technology. At Amazon, you'll grow into the high-impact engineer you know you can be, supported by a culture of learning and mentorship. Every day brings exciting new challenges and opportunities for personal growth.


Amazon is committed to a diverse and inclusive workplace. Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, protected veteran status, disability, age, or other legally protected status.

Our compensation reflects the cost of labor in Brazil geographic markets. The base pay for this full-time, permanent position ranges from BRL R$9,500 to BRL R$ 10,500 per month (in addition to Amazon Restricted Stock Units and Sign-on Payments in Year 1 and Year 2).

Key job responsibilities
BASIC QUALIFICATIONS
• Currently enrolled in Bachelor's degree or above in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Data Science, Information Systems.
• Completed Bachelor's or Graduate degree in specified fields (Last 24months)
• Knowledge of at least one general-purpose programming language such as Java, Python, C++, C#, Go, Rust, or TypeScript
• Knowledge of one or more of the following:
Data structures implementation; Basic algorithm development; Object-oriented design principles ;Must be 18 years of age or older

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS
• Previous technical internship(s) or demonstrated project experience
• Knowledge of one or more of the following:
• AI tools for development productivity
• Cloud platforms (preferably AWS)
• Database systems (SQL and NoSQL)
• Contributing to open-source projects
• Version control systems
• Debugging and troubleshooting complex systems
• Strong problem-solving and analytical skills
• Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English
• Demonstrated ability to learn and adapt to new technologies quickly
• Basic understanding of software development lifecycle (SDLC)

A day in the life
If selected, you will receive financial relocation support, if currently living 80 kilometers (kms) or more from our São Paulo location.

Office Address:
Avenida Chedid Jafet, 200, Vila Olímpia
São Paulo - SP, 04551-065, Brasil.

About the team
Recruiting process:
Application > Technical Coding Online Assessment > Technical Coding Phone Screen > Three (3) Final Technical Coding & Behavioral Interviews in English.

Recruiting instructions:
Within two (2) business days of successfully submitting your application, you will automatically receive our SDE Full-time Technical Coding Online Assessment. Please take time to study/practice in advance and ***complete within the deadline*** instructed by the assessment.
IMPORTANT: You will be required to complete two sections 1) Technical Coding with the programming language of your preference, and 2) work-behavioral assessment. Make sure to complete both parts.

Technical Coding Preparation Resources:
[IMPORTANT] Technical Coding Preparation Resources:
As we want applicants to be successful in their hiring process, we've compiled the following preparation resources for timely review, which can be taken any time and day. We strongly encourage applicants interested in this opportunity of employment to study and practice as much as possible. jobs.title: Full-Time Software Development Engineer, São Paulo

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 4+ years of working in mechanical engineering or equivalent experience
- Experience in 3D CAD
- Bachelor's degree in mechanical engineering or equivalent
- Ability to build complex, well-structured parametric models and assemblies efficiently and cleanly
- Demonstrated experience designing injection molded parts for production, including DFM analysis and GD&T
- Direct experience managing tooling development with overseas vendors including tool design reviews, sampling, and FAI
- Strong 2D drawing skills with working knowledge of GD&T (ASME Y14.5)
jobs.description:
As a Design Engineer, you will own the detailed design and development of mechanical components and assemblies from concept through production release. This is a highly CAD-intensive role. You'll spend your time designing parts, building and maintaining assemblies, and working through the full DFM and tooling process with our overseas manufacturing partners. You'll work closely with our industrial design, mechanical engineering, and manufacturing teams to deliver parts that are beautiful, functional, and manufacturable.

Key job responsibilities
Part Design & CAD
- Own detailed 3D modeling and 2D drawing creation for mechanical components and assemblies in Onshape
- Design parts across a range of manufacturing processes with a primary focus on injection molding and CNC machining
- Maintain clean, well-structured CAD models and assemblies with robust parametric relationships and change-tolerant architecture
- Manage drawing packages including GD&T callouts, tolerances, and surface finish specifications appropriate for the manufacturing process
- Support design iterations rapidly through prototype and EVT/DVT build cycles, incorporating feedback from testing and manufacturing

Injection Molding & Tooling
- Lead DFM reviews for injection molded parts, identifying and resolving issues related to draft, wall thickness, sink, warp, parting lines, and gate location
- Own the tooling development process end-to-end: RFQ, vendor selection, tool design review, T0/T1/T2 sampling, and first article approval
- Manage relationships with tooling vendors — communicating design intent clearly across time zones , tracking tool build progress, and driving issues to resolution
- Review and redline tooling designs, mold flow analyses, and DFM feedback from vendors
- Coordinate with supply chain on tooling lead times, costs, and production readiness

Cross-Functional Collaboration
- Work closely with industrial designers to translate aesthetic intent into manufacturable geometry without compromising the design vision
- Partner with electrical and systems engineers to integrate PCBs, connectors, cables, and other components into enclosures and structural assemblies
- Collaborate with quality and manufacturing teams to develop inspection criteria and first article requirements for molded parts
- Support tolerance stack-up analysis and fit/function verification during builds

About the team
Fauna Robotics, an Amazon company, is building capable, safe, and genuinely delightful robots for everyday life. Our goal is simple: make robots people actually want to live and interact with in everyday human spaces.
We believe that future won’t arrive until building for robotics becomes far more accessible. Today, too much effort is spent reinventing the fundamentals. We’re changing that by developing tightly integrated hardware and software systems that make it faster, safer, and more intuitive to create real-world robotic products.
Our work spans the full stack: mechanical design, control systems, dynamic modeling, and intelligent software. The focus is not just functionality, but experience. We’re building robots that feel responsive, expressive, and genuinely useful.
At Fauna, you’ll work at the frontier of this space, helping define how robots move, manipulate, and interact with people in natural environments. It’s an opportunity to solve hard problems across hardware and software with a team focused on making robotics accessible and joyful to build.
If you care about making robotics real for everyone and building systems that are as delightful as they are capable, we’re interested in hearing from you.

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Bachelor's degree
- Experience owning/driving roadmap strategy and definition
- Experience in representing and advocating for a variety of critical customers and stakeholders during executive-level prioritization and planning
- 5+ years of hands-on SAP MDG configuration experience with a minimum of 2 full end-to-end implementations, preferably in a manufacturing environment with a strong focus on Material Master.
- Experience implementing and supporting SAP MDG solutions, including data modeling, governance frameworks, and workflow configuration.
- Strong understanding of data governance principles, data quality management, and master data lifecycle processes.
- Experience integrating SAP MDG with SAP S/4HANA and core modules such as MM, PP, EWM, QM, and Finance.
- Hands-on experience with BRF+, workflow design, change request management, and data replication frameworks.
- Experience in troubleshooting data issues, workflow errors, and integration challenges.
jobs.description:
Amazon Leo is Amazon’s low Earth orbit satellite broadband network. Its mission is to deliver fast, reliable internet to customers and communities around the world, and we’ve designed the system with the capacity, flexibility, and performance to serve a wide range of customers, from individual households to schools, hospitals, businesses, government agencies, and other organizations operating in locations without reliable connectivity.

Export Control Requirement:
Due to applicable export control laws and regulations, candidates must be a U.S. citizen or national, U.S. permanent resident (i.e., current Green Card holder), or lawfully admitted into the U.S. as a refugee or granted asylum.

As a SAP MDG Product Manager, you will maintain and develop the MDG functionality for Leo Production Systems keeping in mind requirements from customers and other stakeholders. This role will be responsible for building a long-term data Master Data Governance System aimed at improving process efficiency and master data accuracy.

Key job responsibilities
Define and drive the enterprise Master Data Governance (MDG) strategy by translating business needs into scalable system solutions and leading activities across design, build, testing, training, and deployment. Establish and enforce data governance standards, policies, and procedures to ensure high-quality, consistent, and compliant material master data across LEO, monitored through defined KPIs and SLAs.

Configure and support SAP MDG for Material Master, including data models, governance structures, validation rules, and workflows to enable controlled data creation and maintenance. Develop and maintain business rules, approval processes, and change request workflows to ensure accuracy, traceability, and compliance across the material lifecycle.

Design, optimize, and support workflow processes while troubleshooting issues related to approvals, data replication failures, and process inefficiencies. Ensure seamless integration of MDG with SAP S/4HANA, core modules, and non-SAP systems to maintain reliable data distribution and consistency.

Collaborate with technical teams on BRF+, UI modeling, and data replication frameworks, while analyzing and delivering new business requirements and enhancements. Partner with cross-functional stakeholders to drive execution, resolve data and integration issues, and continuously improve governance practices through standardization and automation.

A day in the life
Enable seamless integration of MDG with SAP S/4HANA, MM, EWM, QM, PP, Finance, and non-SAP systems, ensuring reliable data distribution and consistency across platforms.
Govern the material lifecycle by enforcing standardization, harmonization, and compliance across all stages of material master data management.

jobs.description:
We are a passionate team of doers that apply advances in Supply Chain technology to solve real-world problems and transform our customers’ experiences in ways we can’t even imagine yet.

As an System Development Engineer, Supply Chain Technology, you will be working with a unique and gifted team and partnering with cross-functional teams. You will be primary owner for designing, developing and implementing and maintaining the ERP extensions and customizations, working with the ERP tech spec along with AWS components like React, Node JS and JavaScript.

You will be a catalyst for effective teamwork, communication, collaboration, and will invent mechanisms and processes for speed and scale in our context. Our fast-paced team offers great opportunities for self-driven and results-oriented individuals who are not afraid of big challenges, love to innovate, are focused on excellent customer experiences, and can successfully complex drive programs from start to finish.

Key job responsibilities
- Design, build and extend applications. Design and develop solutions and maintain multiple applications within an ERP system and open source technologies as required.
- Develop technical specifications.
- Configure standard applications.
- Developing and implementing UI components using React.js
- Partnering with cross-functional teams to design and ship new features
- Building reusable components and front-end libraries for future use
- Translating designs and wireframes into high-quality code
- Optimizing components for maximum performance across various web browsers
- Staying up-to-date with the latest industry trends and technologies
- Troubleshooting and debugging issues to ensure smooth user experiences
- Participating in code reviews to maintain code quality and consistency



A day in the life
On any given day, the team member will work on a variety of items, including but not limited to, troubleshooting, perform enhancements, write out documentation, attend meeting and keep up with new technologies.

About the team
You will be a member of the Engineering team whose mission is to delight customers in their use of ERP via functionality and ease of use. We are looking for team members who can ask probing questions when dealing with customers and at the same time, delight the customers with a quick turnaround. New ideas are always welcomed as well as the willingness to be a team player.

Our ERP instance uses the Supply Chain modules and the main customers are Amazon’s OpsTech Solutions- Supply Chain (OTS-SC) with additional customers coming from outside OTS-SC. Our customer are global in nature and the ability to see big picture in terms of ERP will come in handy. As we branch out into the AWS tool set, your knowledge of these tools will be a great addition to the team.
